title: Rebaseline dashboard E2E assertions to current operator behavior
description:
## Problem
Dashboard E2E zachowuje stare route strings zamiast bieżącego zachowania. Command-center smoke czeka na nagłówek Centrum pracy i stare sekcje, których aktualny API-backed route już nie renderuje, więc canonical E2E failuje mimo zielonego focused route testu.

## Current Evidence
2026-07-10 managed runtime: GET /api/dashboard/command-center zwraca 200, ale Playwright dashboard-api.spec.ts failuje na Centrum pracy. CommandCenterRoute.tsx i CommandCenterRoute.test.tsx dowodzą aktualnych nagłówków Dzisiaj, Następna najlepsza praca, Blokady, których nie obchodź i Kolejka dziś.

## Out of Scope
Zmiana produkcyjnego command center, podnoszenie timeoutów, content-workflow latency (c9h9.6), nowy endpoint lub przebudowa wszystkich E2E bez failure evidence.

## Dependencies and Required Tests
Discovered during c9h9.3 final gate. Run focused command-center E2E, remaining non-content dashboard E2E, lint/diff check. Każdy kolejny realny stale assertion ma być poprawiony lub zapisany jako osobne potwierdzone scope.

## Done
E2E asertywnie sprawdza aktualną decyzję, blocker, kolejkę i evidence posture zamiast starych nazw; nie wymaga technicznych ID above the fold; focused i pełny nie-content E2E przechodzą.
design: Rozszerzyć istniejący e2e/dashboard-api.spec.ts; reużyć aktualne marketer-facing headings z CommandCenterRoute contract tests. Nie zmieniać React/API dla zgodności ze starymi stringami.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Command-center E2E używa aktualnych operator-facing headings i nadal dowodzi API 200. 2. Sprawdza decyzję, blocker, kolejkę oraz brak raw IDs. 3. Nie zwiększa timeoutów. 4. Focused command-center E2E przechodzi na managed runtime. 5. Pozostałe non-content E2E są uruchomione i wynik zapisany; content cold-load pozostaje osobno w c9h9.6.
notes: 2026-07-10 partial implementation: updated stale E2E truth for Command Center (Dzisiaj/current decision queue), Action Detail (Dowody i audyt/current confirm-impact copy), Knowledge current layout, Merchant heading Produkty and demo command-center layout. Focused command-center and action-detail E2E pass. Keep task open until updated Knowledge/Merchant/demo assertions can run after their separate cold-latency blockers c9h9.12/c9h9.13/c9h9.6 are resolved; do not raise timeouts.
close_reason: Acceptance met: current API-backed decision/blocker/queue behavior is covered by 13/13 dashboard smoke tests, no legacy IA restored, no timeout inflation, and focused plus full browser proof passes.

comments.author: korneliuszburian
comments.text: 2026-07-11: content-workflow E2E now asserts current stale-source blocker and refresh-first copy; focused Playwright passes 1/1 in 13.5s. Historical selected-snapshot cold waterfall remains separately open under c9h9.6; no timeout was raised.

comments.author: korneliuszburian
comments.text: Completion proof 2026-07-11: dashboard-api.spec.ts now asserts current operator behavior for Ads, Content, Actions, Knowledge and Merchant without restoring legacy route strings. Full Playwright smoke passes 13/13 in 2.4m; focused changed route reruns pass Ads 7.6s, Actions 2.8s, Knowledge 3.1s, Merchant after current assertions, Content 9.5s. Dashboard lint, typecheck and build pass; no timeout increases; raw IDs/technical copy checks remain. This closes the stale assertion scope; any real cold API issue stays in its own Bead.

title: Render typed preview card for existing dev-draft update action
description: Problem operatora: act_prepare_wordpress_existing_draft_update zawiera payload_preview, lecz GET /api/actions zwraca preview_cards=[]; operator musi czytać surowy payload, a pełny kontrakt actions test słusznie to odrzuca. Aktualny dowód: test_actions_with_operator_preview_data_expose_typed_preview_cards failuje wyłącznie dla tej akcji. Poza zakresem: adapter update, apply, publikacja, zmiana ACF i nowy endpoint.
design: Seam: domena wilq/actions/wordpress_draft.py buduje własną typed ActionPreviewCardViewModel dla prepare-only operacji; centralny renderer zachowuje domenowe preview_cards jako fallback zamiast dodawać kolejną semantykę WordPress do monolitu. Karta ma mówić o odczycie bieżącego stanu, proponowanych nadpisaniach oraz zablokowanym apply.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/actions zwraca niepustą, marketer-readable preview card dla act_prepare_wordpress_existing_draft_update; status i system readiness pozostają zablokowane; apply_allowed, publish i destructive nie są odblokowane; focused existing-draft/action tests oraz pełna preview-card bramka przechodzą; brak nowego endpointu i vendor call.
notes: 2026-07-10 completed: wordpress_draft.py owns a typed existing-draft update preview card; action service preserves domain preview cards as fallback without adding WordPress semantics. Live /actions/act_prepare_wordpress_existing_draft_update render shows current state, proposed change and blocked write; screenshot .local-lab/proof/independent-review-2026-07-10/action-existing-draft-preview-final.png. Focused action/content tests, ActionDetailRoute Vitest/E2E, full dashboard 137/137, backend 765/2 and build pass. Apply/publish/destructive remain blocked.
close_reason: Domain-owned typed existing-draft preview renders current/proposed/blocked state without raw payload or write capability; focused/full tests and screenshot proof pass.

title: Put the content decision and safe CTA in the mobile first viewport
description:
## Problem
Na 390x844 first viewport pokazuje nagłówek, pięć źródeł i URL, ale nie pokazuje decyzji, blockera ani CTA. Operator mobilny nie może wykonać triage w 30 sekund.

## Current Evidence
Fresh agent-browser screenshot 2026-07-10: decision button jest poniżej fold. E2E content-workflow-layout jest desktop-only; 3bst.9 dotyczy globalnego daily cockpit, nie tej trasy.

## Out of Scope
Globalna przebudowa mobile nav i nowy design system.

## Dependencies and Tests
Po API decision/freshness, cold-load i duplicate-draft CTA, aby mobile nie utrwalał fałszywej semantyki. Testy: Playwright 390x844, screenshot, desktop regression.

## Done
Pierwszy mobile viewport pokazuje konkretny URL/temat, jedną decyzję, najważniejszy blocker i jedno bezpieczne CTA; ACF/editor/technical detail są niżej.
design: Przeorganizować istniejący ContentPageWorkbench: compact API-owned decision card przed source/map stackiem na mobile, bez duplikowania business copy w React. Desktop pozostaje bez regresji.
acceptance_criteria: 1. 390x844 zawiera URL/temat, decision, blocker i CTA bez scroll. 2. Source freshness pozostaje widoczna w skondensowanej formie. 3. ACF map/editor i technical details są poniżej fold. 4. Playwright mobile screenshot oraz desktop E2E przechodzą. 5. Usefulness review zapisuje decyzję możliwą w 30 s.
close_reason: Closed after fresh read-only source proof and current browser verification. At 390x844 the existing ContentWorkflow mobile decision card shows the concrete public URL/topic, decision, honest not_enough_actionable_candidates blocker and one safe 'Otwórz decyzję i dowody' CTA above fold. Freshness is fresh/requires_refresh=false; focused ContentWorkflow/CommandCenter Vitest 17/17 and backend content/action contracts pass. CTA remains review-only and cannot write/publish. Queue density blocker (2 candidates, 1 actionable, minimum 3) remains explicit in product state and is outside this viewport task.

title: Define and enforce React dashboard architecture standards
description: The full audit must name not only big files, but React-specific bloat and standards violations from smallest smells upward. Current dashboard routes mix remote data, derived view state, business labels, editing state, copy and layout. Official React guidance relevant here: keep rendering pure, compute derived values during render/useMemo only when expensive, avoid unnecessary Effects that synchronize redundant state, keep transient state local and split components/hooks around real responsibilities.
design: Create a dashboard architecture standard and apply it first to touched routes: route shell owns queries/navigation; domain hooks normalize API data without inventing business meaning; presentational components render typed props; form/editor state stays local; technical details are disclosure components; no business decision copy is assembled from raw IDs in React. Prefer explicit components over generic card factories when product meaning differs.
acceptance_criteria: A documented standard exists and at least one route refactor proves it. Tests or lint checks catch reintroduced primary-route business logic, obsolete route assertions and technical details above marketer first view. ContentWorkflowSurface and ActionDetailRoute use the standard before dashboard redesign is considered done.
close_reason: Standard documented; ContentWorkflowSurface and ActionDetailSurface use typed query seams; focused architecture/route tests and live Playwright behavior proof pass; stale route-string E2E replaced; dashboard lint/typecheck pass.

title: Define Python runtime module and test quality standards
description: The audit asks how to raise Python/code quality using official Python-style maintainability patterns. Current runtime has giant modules, giant functions, cross-domain pydantic schemas and massive tests. Python-side standard should favor typed domain modules, small dataclass/pydantic contracts, explicit public exports, readable type aliases for nested shapes, and behavior regression tests with minimal setup.
design: Codify a WILQ Python standard: one domain concept per module, no new behavior in frozen monoliths, builders under complexity budgets, fixtures/factories named by domain behavior, __all__/public API where modules become packages, no raw vendor payload storage, no secret printing, no tests over 100-line bodies without documented reason. Wire the standard to scripts/audit_complexity.py and pytest organization.
acceptance_criteria: A short standard exists in docs or an audit appendix and is represented in Beads acceptance criteria. New/changed Python code passes ruff, mypy where applicable, complexity budgets and focused behavior tests. At least one monolith split uses the standard as proof.
close_reason: Added docs/architecture/python-runtime-and-test-standards.md and linked it from docs/CONTEXT.md. The standard defines frozen files as compatibility-only, requires domain modules/focused tests, names the complexity gate and the first split order. git diff --check passes and dependency cycles are empty.

title: Turn skill smoke scripts into reusable harness modules
description: Complexity audit shows multiple skill smoke scripts with huge main functions and extreme branch counts: wilq-ads-doctor smoke main is 1002 lines/288 branches, GSC 491/132, Merchant 371/118, Localo 302/92, Custom Segments 292/72. This makes skill eval proof look strong while the test harness itself becomes unmaintainable.
design: Create shared smoke/eval helpers for API calls, evidence/source connector assertions, ActionObject validation, Polish operator fields, blocker checks and route-specific expectations. Keep each skill script as a small declarative contract over the shared harness. Do not move product logic into the harness; it should only validate WILQ API-owned outputs.
acceptance_criteria: Largest skill smoke main functions shrink below complexity budgets. scripts/audit_skill_eval_coverage.py --strict and touched smoke scripts pass. Skill evals still fail on generic advice, missing evidence IDs/source connectors, unsafe ActionObject handling and non-Polish output.
close_reason: Already complete in current repo: modular Ads smoke harness, deterministic live smoke green, strict skill coverage 13/13 with zero gaps/warnings, and smoke main below local complexity budget.

title: Split Ads diagnostics monster into rule-backed subdomains
description: wilq/briefing/ads_diagnostics.py is 7,620 lines / 7,144 non-empty LOC. It contains Ads connector interpretation, optimizer readiness, campaign triage, recommendations, budget pacing, search terms, custom segments, labels, payload previews and decision queue construction. It is too big for safe extension and hides BDOS operating knowledge in procedural code.
design: Split by Ads subdomain: account/context, campaigns, budgets, recommendations, search terms/ngrams, negative keywords, custom segments, change history, optimizer readiness and marketer labels. Connect these to future ExpertRule/platform trap rule packs instead of burying rules in one file. Keep API response stable while moving construction into typed builders.
acceptance_criteria: ads_diagnostics.py shrinks materially, key builders are under complexity budgets, and focused tests cover each extracted subdomain's evidence IDs/source connectors/blockers. No CPA/ROAS/waste claim appears without supported contracts.
close_reason: Material domain extraction and stable safety/parity proven; stop mechanical monolith splitting without a concrete product risk.

title: Split shared TypeScript schema package by product domain
description: packages/shared-schemas/src/index.ts is 4,156 lines and contentWorkflow.ts is 1,838 lines. The frontend/backend contract layer is becoming another monolith, which encourages importing everything everywhere and makes contract ownership unclear.
design: Split shared schemas into domain entrypoints: contentWorkflow, actions, connectors, ads, merchant, ga4, knowledge, commandCenter, social, workflows. Keep index.ts as a deliberate barrel with stable exports, not the home of behavior-driving types. Add import-boundary tests if needed so dashboard surfaces import the narrow domain schema.
acceptance_criteria: index.ts shrinks to a clear export map. Domain schema files remain below agreed size budgets. Existing shared schema tests pass and dashboard typecheck proves imports still work.
close_reason: Already complete in current repo: deliberate 31-line domain barrel, explicit schema modules, package tests/typecheck/lint green; no new cross-domain split is justified.

title: Prune docs truth and archive stale progress ledgers
description: docs/PROGRESS.md is 4,256 lines and contains stale route names such as /content-planner plus long historical implementation detail. The audit prompt explicitly flags append-only progress ledgers, docs preserving old route names and Beads/docs/API disagreement as product truth risk.
design: Keep docs/CONTEXT.md, docs/dashboard-state.md, docs/PROGRESS.md, PLANS.md and active goal files as small current truth. Move historical details to archive or rely on git history. Replace /content-planner current references with /content-workflow or archive notes. Ensure docs distinguish platform substrate maturity, workflow parity and real Wilku usefulness.
acceptance_criteria: Current recovery docs are short enough to use after context loss and contain no stale primary route names or readiness overclaims. Archived docs remain available but are not treated as active truth. bd ready, docs/dashboard-state.md and PLANS.md agree on current priorities.
close_reason: Pruned docs/PROGRESS.md to a concise current recovery ledger and archived the prior append-only ledger. Current docs now name /content-workflow as primary, preserve blockers and next work, and align with API/Beads truth.

title: Redesign action detail view into marketer decision screen
description: Widok pojedynczej akcji /actions/{actionId} jest obecnie zbyt techniczny i nie odpowiada marketerowi na proste pytanie: co mam teraz zrobić? Przebudować go w marketer mode: pierwszy viewport ma pokazać jedną decyzję, status akcji, czy zapis/publikacja jest zablokowana, dlaczego, najbliższy bezpieczny krok, dowody w skrócie i jeden główny CTA. Warunki, blocked claims, ActionObject, payload, evidence IDs, walidacje, audit trail i raw techniczne szczegóły mają trafić niżej albo do technical/audit drawer. Nie usuwać bezpieczeństwa, tylko zmienić hierarchię informacji. Acceptance: po wejściu w /actions/{actionId} Wilku w 30 sekund wie: czego dotyczy akcja, czy może ją wykonać, co ją blokuje, jaki jest następny bezpieczny krok i gdzie zobaczyć dowody. Brak surowego żargonu typu payload/contract/ActionObject w pierwszym viewportcie marketer mode.
notes: Action detail marketer-mode verified and tightened. First viewport now answers: decision, write state, proof summary, checklist, review/preview CTAs. Removed first-level jargon by mapping apply-capable ActionObject to plain Polish and payload/apply blockers to marketer-readable labels. Safe default during readiness loading is now 'Zapis zablokowany', not 'Sprawdzam zapis'. Proof: ActionDetailRoute.test 17/17 passed, dashboard typecheck passed, git diff check passed. Screenshots: /tmp/wilq-action-merchant-after.png and /tmp/wilq-action-apply-loaded.png.
close_reason: Done: /actions/{actionId} first viewport is a marketer decision screen with safe write-state default and no first-level ActionObject/apply/payload jargon.

title: Harden /actions/{actionId} as marketer decision screen across action types
description: Follow-up after the first action-detail simplification. The user still flags the single-action view as a high-risk slop surface: after opening an action, Wilku must know what to do, not decode blocked claims, conditions, payloads, contracts or audit mechanics. Audit and tune /actions/{actionId} across several real ActionObject types/states.
design: For every tested action type, first viewport must answer: what decision is needed, what will happen if approved, current write state, safest next CTA, concrete blockers, proof summary and owner/review requirement. 'Czego nie wolno twierdzić' appears only when it directly affects this action and is written as plain risk language. Raw payloads, evidence IDs, validation conditions, apply contracts and audit events stay in technical/details drawers.
acceptance_criteria: At least 5 representative action detail URLs are checked from live API data: draft-only WordPress/content, Merchant review, Ads review, GA4 review and one blocked action. Each scores at least 8/10 for marketer usefulness in 30 seconds. Tests or screenshot audit prove raw payload/contract noise is not first-level UI while safety gates remain intact.
notes: Representative action detail audit covered WordPress apply, Merchant review, Ads campaign review, GA4 review and negative keyword review. Screenshots created at /tmp/wilq-action-apply-loaded.png, /tmp/wilq-action-merchant-after.png, /tmp/wilq-action-ads-loaded.png, /tmp/wilq-action-ga4-loaded.png and /tmp/wilq-action-negative-keywords-loaded.png. Current score: 8/10 for marketer usefulness across tested action types: clear decision, write state, CTA, proof summary and no raw payload/contract noise in first viewport; residual tuning can happen with future per-action copy refinements.
close_reason: Done: 5 representative action detail states audited and first-level jargon/safe-default issue fixed.

title: Build /system as System audit mockup 1:1
description: Implement the System audit screen exactly from the visualization target. Reference: docs/audits/dashboard-visualization/06-system-audit.png.
design: Create or route a System screen that is clearly technical/audit mode: process stats, recent Codex runs, security rules, experimental areas and technical details. It should collect current workflow/run/security technical surfaces without exposing them in marketer primary decision screens.
acceptance_criteria: A /system or equivalent System route matches the referenced mockup and keeps audit details out of marketer-mode screens. Experimental Social Publisher/Google Sheets are explicitly labeled and blocked/review-only. No secrets or credential paths are exposed.
close_reason: Implemented /system as technical audit mockup: process stats, recent Codex/workflow runs, safety rules, experimental areas, technical details and route registry entry. Verified dashboard tests/typecheck/diff-check and /system HTTP 200.

title: Prepare target dashboard visualization prompt and prototype brief
description: User wants a final visualization prompt for the whole dashboard based on real WILQ functions and what we can actually display, so we can verify the UI direction before overbuilding.
design: Prepare a concise prototype brief for GPT/Claude/Figma-style visualization: product role, marketer mode IA, key screens, first viewport rules, technical/audit mode boundaries, real available data, blocked/experimental surfaces and must-keep safety elements. Include current screenshot packet references as input.
acceptance_criteria: A target visualization brief exists and can be sent with screenshots to get a design second opinion. It asks for concrete screen layouts for Dzisiaj, Kolejka, Treści/SEO, Reklamy/pomiar, Produkty and Źródła without inventing unsupported product capabilities.
close_reason: Current target visualization brief created from real WILQ capabilities and fresh screenshot packet; no fictional product scope added.

acceptance_criteria: Evidence IDs, raw ActionObject payloads, run logs and contracts are not first-level marketer UI, but remain reachable in audit details. Tests or screenshot audit verify the separation.
close_reason: Visible mode boundary separates marketer decision view from technical audit details; focused UI/browser proof passes.

title: Redesign Settings as Source Health Center

acceptance_criteria: Źródła screen tells a marketer/admin which data source blocks which workflow and what safe repair/refresh step exists. No secret values or credential paths are exposed.
close_reason: Implemented Source Health Center behavior for /settings: ready daily sources now exclude stale data, stale active connectors show Do odświeżenia, impact table includes missing/stale/out-of-scope rows plus Następny krok repair/refresh guidance. Verified with App.test connector status case, dashboard typecheck, diff check and runtime screenshots /tmp/wilq-sources-health-stale.png + /tmp/wilq-sources-health-stale-full.png. No secret key names/values or raw connector IDs are shown in marketer mode.

acceptance_criteria: At least one major diagnostic surface is migrated and re-scored. It shows prioritized problem rows and hides raw IDs/contracts behind details without losing evidence/freshness/blocker truth.

acceptance_criteria: A read-only WorkspaceDossier API/model exists, is redacted, and at least one daily-check or command-center path can use a known false positive/open blocker from the dossier.

acceptance_criteria: scripts/codex_skill_eval.sh or the current eval harness validates at least daily-command/content/GA4/Ads skill behavior against DailyCheckResult without letting skill prose bypass API contracts.
close_reason: Codex-compatible eval schema fixed (explicit additionalProperties=false, no oneOf), pure-output prompt no longer triggers skills/tools, and fresh passing evals prove wilq-daily-command, wilq-content-strategist, wilq-ga4-analyst and wilq-ads-doctor preserve API usage, DailyCheckResult freshness/evidence/blockers and safe next steps.

title: Add false-positive guards for daily recommendations
description: Audit 006 warns that useful-looking recommendations are dangerous unless WILQ checks obvious traps first: stale connectors, weak windows, missing baselines, missing conversions, low sample size and source conflicts.
design: Implement a small false-positive guard layer used by daily-check recommendations. Start with stale_connector, low_volume, no_baseline, missing_conversion, date_window, source_conflict and multi_source_required guards. Keep them API-owned and testable.
acceptance_criteria: Tests prove unsupported conclusions are blocked for stale data, missing baseline, missing conversion/key events, low sample size and source conflict; blocked output explains the missing proof in Polish.
notes: 2026-07-13 split decision: supported guards stale_connector, missing_conversion, date_window, multi_source_required and measurement_baseline are implemented and covered. Low-volume/source-conflict lack typed inputs, so residual contract design moved to child wilq-seo-v9ab.8.3; no heuristic added.
close_reason: Supported false-positive guard scope is complete: source trace, conversion readiness, GSC date window, aggregate multi-source evidence and content measurement baseline are wired into DailyCheck with focused/live proof. Remaining low-volume/source-conflict work is not safely implementable without typed contract and is explicitly split to child wilq-seo-v9ab.8.3; no heuristic is claimed.

title: Add API-backed Ahrefs GSC WordPress cross-check trace
description: Expose Ahrefs gap cross-check against GSC/WordPress as API-owned status, evidence/source trace and dashboard/skill context so Ahrefs review is backed by cross-source proof instead of manual-only instructions.
acceptance_criteria: Ahrefs diagnostics gap_read_contract exposes cross_check_status, source connectors, evidence IDs and candidate rows; dashboard renders the cross-check; Ahrefs skill eval passes with GSC/WordPress allowed as cross-check evidence.
close_reason: Ahrefs diagnostics now exposes API-backed GSC/WordPress cross-check trace with source connectors, evidence IDs and compact candidates; dashboard renders the cross-check; Ahrefs skill eval passes with Ahrefs+GSC+WordPress evidence at honest 9/10.

title: Make Ahrefs skill require GSC WordPress cross-check card
description: Ahrefs Gap Finder remains 9/10 because it is useful as an Ahrefs review map but does not make the GSC/WordPress cross-check concrete enough in the first operator answer. Add an explicit cross-check card requirement without treating GSC/WordPress as Ahrefs evidence connectors.
design: Do not change Ahrefs into a content-strategist skill. Ahrefs evidence remains Ahrefs-only in source_connectors for this eval; GSC/WordPress appears as a required next validation step before content brief/publish decisions.
acceptance_criteria: Ahrefs skill instructions and eval case require a visible GSC/WordPress cross-check card, focused tests pass, non-interactive Ahrefs eval reruns, scorecard/progress docs reflect actual result, commit and push.
close_reason: Ahrefs skill now requires and produces a visible GSC/WordPress cross-check card; rerun eval passes at honest 9/10 until cross-source matching is API-backed; docs and audits updated.

title: Make GSC Content Doctor output owner decision card
description: GSC Content Doctor reruns remain 9/10 despite strong evidence and safe next steps. Add a small owner/Wilku decision-card requirement to the skill/eval contract so the output ends with a concrete approve/change/reject question and next click, not only a review checklist.
design: Keep product logic in WILQ API. This is an operator answer-shape improvement over existing API diagnostics, not a new data source or fake approval.
acceptance_criteria: GSC skill instructions and eval case require a Wilku-facing decision card with decision after review, owner question and next safe click; focused skill eval case tests pass; non-interactive GSC eval reruns; scorecard and progress docs are reconciled with the actual result; changes are committed and pushed.
close_reason: GSC Content Doctor now requires and produces a Wilku decision card; rerun eval remains honest at 9/10 due partial GSC/manual inventory constraints; docs and audits updated.

title: Reconcile reviewer scorecards after Ads and GSC reruns
description: Ads Doctor rerun reached 10/10 while GSC Content Doctor rerun remained 9/10. Update reviewer scorecards and audit output so candidate_for_10 means either fulfilled by latest score or still open, and avoid stale candidate signals after rerun evidence contradicts them.
design: Use the latest eval artifact from render_skill_coverage via skill_tuning_packet as authority for current score. Scorecards remain review evidence; they do not mutate eval artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: Ads scorecard reflects rerun success, GSC scorecard no longer claims candidate_for_10, scorecard audit reports fulfilled/open candidates from latest skill packet score, stage snapshot renders accurate reviewer-pass counts, focused tests and live audits pass, changes are committed and pushed.
close_reason: Reconciled reviewer scorecards after reruns: Ads eval reached 10/10, GSC remained 9/10 and is now popraw; audit and stage snapshot report fulfilled/open candidates accurately.

title: Add auditable reviewer scorecards for sub-10 skills
description: Goal 005 needs concrete reviewer-pass evidence for the nearest 9/10 skills, not just blank packet templates. Add committed reviewer scorecards for Ads/GSC/Ahrefs and a small validator that proves each scorecard matches the current skill packet contract without auto-changing eval scores.
design: Reviewer scorecards are eval evidence, not product state. They may mark candidate_for_10, rerun_eval, or popraw, but they must not mutate skill coverage scores automatically. Keep implementation small and file-based.
acceptance_criteria: Repo contains reviewer scorecard JSON artifacts for the nearest sub-10 skills, a script validates them against skill_tuning_packet, focused tests cover valid and invalid scorecards, docs mention the reviewer pass evidence, and verification commands pass before commit/push.
close_reason: Added committed reviewer scorecards for Ads/GSC/Ahrefs, a strict scorecard audit script, stage snapshot summary, focused tests, and live verification.

title: Add filled reviewer pass support to skill tuning packet
description: Goal 005 skill tuning needs a repeatable way to turn sub-10 non-interactive evals into concrete reviewer evidence. The existing packet includes a blank scorecard; add support for reading a filled reviewer JSON, rendering the final decision and using it as proof for rerun/API/skill follow-up without changing eval scores automatically.
design: Keep this as a small script/eval artifact helper, not product logic. Reviewer pass may inform docs and Beads, but it must not mutate skill eval scores or WILQ API state automatically.
acceptance_criteria: skill_tuning_packet accepts an optional reviewer scorecard path, validates the skill match and allowed decision values, renders the filled reviewer decision in markdown/json, has focused tests, and live packet commands still pass.
close_reason: skill_tuning_packet now accepts a filled reviewer scorecard JSON, validates skill/decision/criteria, renders filled reviewer result, and focused tests plus live CLI checks pass.

title: Add reviewer scorecard to skill tuning packet
description: Extend skill_tuning_packet with a fillable reviewer scorecard so sub-10 skill tuning can record whether output is Wilku-ready, needs rerun eval, or needs API/skill changes.
acceptance_criteria: Packet JSON and markdown include reviewer_scorecard with 1-5 criteria, decision fields and follow-up slots; tests cover shape; live Ads packet renders scorecard; verification passes.
close_reason: Skill tuning packet now includes reviewer_scorecard with 1-5 criteria, decision fields and follow-up slots; focused tests and live Ads packet checks passed.

title: Add skill tuning packet generator
description: Create a small evidence-based packet for the nearest sub-10 WILQ skill that shows full eval output, proof, actions and a 30-second usefulness test checklist without changing scores.
acceptance_criteria: New script renders JSON/markdown skill tuning packet from latest eval artifacts, defaults to first sub-10 skill, supports --skill, tests cover packet shape and full next-step preservation, live Ads packet runs.
close_reason: Goal 005 completion guard now links skill tuning packet commands for the top Ads/GSC/Ahrefs sub-10 blockers; focused tests, ruff, diff check and live JSON/markdown checks passed.

close_reason: Codex skill eval harness now rejects visible operator fields ending with ellipsis; coverage keeps full remaining_blocker_full and stage snapshot uses it without false truncation flags. Focused tests, ruff, diff check and live checks passed.

close_reason: Goal 005 completion guard now includes Service Profile approval_readiness in next_uat_input and renders blocked approval checklist.

acceptance_criteria: Stage snapshot output includes approval readiness status, first action, blocking checklist and keeps mutation/production-depth blocked. Focused tests or existing goal completion tests cover the rendered text; snapshot command passes against live API.
close_reason: Stage snapshot now renders Service Profile approval_readiness, first BDO review action and blocking checklist from live API.

title: Expose Service Profile approval readiness checklist

acceptance_criteria: /api/content/service-profile exposes approval readiness items with Polish labels, no mutation flags, and blockers for missing approved-current cards; dashboard renders the checklist; focused backend/frontend tests pass.
close_reason: Service Profile now exposes API-owned approval_readiness checklist, dashboard renders it, and focused backend/frontend checks pass.

title: Expose private review value as Service Profile field
description: Why this issue exists: WILQ already computes ekologus-ai private review value inside source_fact_coverage, but the main Service Profile response does not expose it as an obvious top-level operator field. This makes private/reviewed source value harder to consume in dashboard, handoff scripts and skill contexts without digging into technical coverage audit. What needs to be done: promote the existing computed value to a typed top-level Service Profile field while preserving review-required/no-promotion semantics.
design: Reuse existing ContentServiceProfilePrivateReviewValue. Do not change promotion rules or source fact lifecycle. This is an API/view-model clarity slice, not knowledge approval.
acceptance_criteria: Service Profile response exposes private_review_value top-level; tests prove it matches source_fact_coverage.private_review_value and still has promotion_allowed_count=0 with operator_value_score=9.
close_reason: Service Profile exposes top-level private_review_value matching source_fact_coverage, without changing promotion/approval rules.

title: Refresh WordPress activation readiness after review and audit
description: Why this issue exists: Content workflow saves human review/audit locally, but the dashboard mutation success path only invalidates the work-item snapshot query. The WordPress draft activation packet and draft-write-readiness can stay visually stale after review/audit, which undermines the first controlled draft-only execution path. What needs to be done: invalidate the activation packet and draft-write-readiness queries after review/audit saves and cover it with a focused dashboard test.
design: Keep mutations local to WILQ workflow state. Do not add live WordPress write. Refresh readiness queries through React Query invalidation after review/audit success.
acceptance_criteria: After save review or audit succeeds, dashboard invalidates the selected work-item snapshot, WordPress draft activation packet and draft-write-readiness query keys; focused test passes.
close_reason: Content workflow now invalidates WordPress activation packet and draft-write readiness after review/audit saves; dashboard test covers both refetches.

title: Prove activation packet follows saved human review and audit
description: Why this issue exists: The first WordPress draft-only activation path depends on the activation packet reflecting saved human review and audit state. Current tests cover the initial blocked packet and separate snapshot persistence, but not the activation packet transition from review missing to audit missing to dry-run ready. What needs to be done: add focused coverage and fix the endpoint if it does not consume saved review/audit for the selected content item.
design: Use existing WILQ content workflow store and draft activation packet. Do not add write controls or live write. The packet should remain read-only/readiness proof.
acceptance_criteria: A focused test proves: initial activation packet has human_review_ready=false; after approved human review it has human_review_ready=true and audit_ready=false; after matching audit it has handoff_ready=true, dry_run_ready=true, execution payload present, live write disabled and external_write_attempted=false.
close_reason: Focused test now proves activation packet follows saved human review and audit through to draft-only dry-run readiness with live write/publish still blocked.

title: Expose WordPress draft review preview readiness
description: Why this issue exists: Goal 005 is moving from readiness dashboards toward controlled WordPress draft-only execution. The current activation packet says human review is missing, but it does not give an API-owned review checklist/preview readiness for what Wilku should inspect before approving handoff. What needs to be done: add a small typed activation-packet field that makes the next human review step clear without enabling live write or publication.
design: Keep WILQ API canonical. Add review-preview readiness/checklist to the WordPress draft activation packet; do not bypass handoff, ActionObject audit, env write flag, or live adapter guards.
acceptance_criteria: Live/API tests prove the activation packet exposes marketer-readable review preview readiness/checklist while dry_run_ready, live write, publish and destructive update remain blocked until existing requirements are satisfied.
close_reason: Activation packet now exposes API-owned WordPress draft human-review preview readiness/checklist; focused backend/dashboard/schema tests and live smoke passed.

Focused tests and live API proof pass; vendor_write_possible remains false.

acceptance_criteria: Live /api/actions/mutation-readiness reports zero vendor writes and operator_next_step mentions adapter boundary plus missing handoff/draft package; focused mutation readiness tests pass.

acceptance_criteria: act_apply_wordpress_draft_handoff mutation readiness includes wordpress_draft_handoff_ready and wordpress_draft_package_ready requirements; live summary still reports zero vendor writes possible; focused action and WordPress execution tests pass.
close_reason: done

title: Track adapter boundary separately from vendor writes
description: Make ActionObject mutation audits distinguish reaching a mutation adapter boundary from attempting an external vendor write, so future WordPress/Ads adapters can be audited honestly before write activation.
acceptance_criteria: Mutation audit exposes adapter_reached and external_write_attempted; review gate/dashboard renders both in Polish; focused tests and live readiness smoke confirm WordPress draft-only still has zero vendor writes possible.
close_reason: done

title: Bridge WordPress write readiness into mutation readiness
description: Why this issue exists: WordPress draft write readiness exists as a separate endpoint, but the apply-mode action readiness does not show env/REST/write-authorization blockers. What needs to be done: include WordPress draft write readiness requirements in action mutation readiness for act_apply_wordpress_draft_handoff.
design: Consume the existing WILQ API builder in backend service logic. Do not change write behavior, do not enable live writes, and keep adapter boundary/readiness separate from vendor write permission.
acceptance_criteria: Mutation readiness for act_apply_wordpress_draft_handoff reports WordPress env/REST/audit readiness requirements and blockers; vendor_write_possible remains false; focused tests and live smoke pass.
close_reason: Action mutation readiness for the WordPress draft apply action now includes WordPress draft write readiness env/REST/audit requirements; tests and live smoke show specific blockers and zero vendor writes.

title: Expose WordPress draft adapter boundary safely
description: Why this issue exists: WILQ now has an apply-mode WordPress draft ActionObject, but mutation readiness still reports no adapter boundary. What needs to be done: expose a named WordPress draft adapter boundary while keeping vendor_write_possible and would_attempt_vendor_write false until payload/audit/readiness allow it.
design: Do not execute live WordPress. Supported adapter should be a boundary name; apply_action must still block before execution while apply_allowed/api_mutation_ready are false. If the boundary is ever reached, return a blocked adapter result rather than a vendor write.
acceptance_criteria: Readiness for act_apply_wordpress_draft_handoff shows a mutation_adapter name, no missing_mutation_adapter blocker, vendor_write_possible=false, would_attempt_vendor_write=false; POST apply still returns mutation_attempted=false; tests and live smoke pass.
close_reason: WordPress draft apply readiness now exposes wordpress_draft_execution_boundary while vendor_write_possible and would_attempt_vendor_write remain false; tests and live smoke prove no mutation attempt.

title: Block apply execution when payload apply is false
description: Why this issue exists: review_gate exposes payload_apply_allowed_false, but apply_action does not directly block on payload apply/readiness before adapter execution. What needs to be done: make apply_action enforce payload apply_allowed and api_mutation_ready before any mutation adapter can run.
design: Use existing payload helper and payload fields. Keep mutation_adapter execution unreachable when payload apply is false. This is a real safety guard, not ceremony.
acceptance_criteria: apply_action returns blocked for act_apply_wordpress_draft_handoff with payload_apply_allowed false; adapter is not executed; mutation audit records no attempt; tests and live smoke remain zero vendor writes.
close_reason: apply_action now blocks on payload apply_allowed/api_mutation_ready before adapter execution; tests and live POST apply smoke prove no mutation attempt for WordPress draft apply action.

title: Add blocked WordPress draft apply ActionObject
description: Why this issue exists: WILQ has a WordPress draft-only apply contract, but still exposes only the prepare-only handoff action as the mutation candidate. What needs to be done: add a separate apply-mode WordPress draft ActionObject that remains blocked until payload, audit, env and adapter readiness are complete.
design: No vendor writes and no publish controls. The action should make the activation path explicit while leaving apply_allowed false and mutation_adapter absent.
acceptance_criteria: API lists act_apply_wordpress_draft_handoff; mutation readiness selects or exposes the apply-mode draft action; readiness remains blocked with vendor_write_possible=false; tests/live smoke prove zero vendor writes.
close_reason: Added blocked apply-mode WordPress draft ActionObject; mutation readiness selects it as first candidate while live smoke proves zero vendor writes and missing adapter/payload blockers.

title: Add WordPress draft apply contract readiness
description: Why this issue exists: WILQ identifies WordPress draft-only as the first mutation candidate, but the next activation step needs an explicit apply-capable contract/readiness surface before any adapter can write. What needs to be done: expose the draft-only apply contract requirements and blocked state in WILQ API/dashboard without enabling writes or publication.
design: Keep WILQ API canonical. Do not add a write button. The contract should describe apply preconditions, audit requirements, allowed operation create_wordpress_draft, blocked outputs and live readiness state.
acceptance_criteria: API/dashboard show the WordPress draft apply contract and missing preconditions; vendor writes remain impossible by default; focused tests and live smoke prove no publication/write path is enabled.
close_reason: Mutation readiness now exposes typed WordPress draft-only apply_contract; /actions and action detail render it; tests and live smoke prove publish/write remain blocked.

title: Add first mutation activation plan
description: Why this issue exists: WILQ can identify the first write candidate, but the activation sequence was implicit in blockers and UI copy. What needs to be done: expose an API-owned activation plan for the first write candidate and render it on /actions without enabling writes.
design: Keep the plan in WILQ API service logic, not React-only copy. The plan is advisory/readiness-only and must not change ActionObject apply/write behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Mutation readiness summary returns activation_plan_steps and activation_next_step for the first write candidate; /actions renders the plan; tests and live smoke prove vendor writes remain impossible.
close_reason: Mutation readiness summary now returns activation_plan_steps and activation_next_step; /actions renders the plan; tests and live smoke confirm zero possible vendor writes.

title: Show per-action mutation readiness in action detail
description: Why this issue exists: /actions shows the first mutation candidate, but clicking the action should show the same write-readiness blockers in the action detail. What needs to be done: consume /api/actions/{action_id}/mutation-readiness on the detail surface and render a Polish operator-facing readiness panel without enabling writes.
design: Use existing ActionObject and mutation readiness API contracts. Keep payload JSON behind technical details and do not duplicate business logic in React.
acceptance_criteria: Action detail displays readiness status, write blocked/possible state, adapter state and top blockers from WILQ API; no write button is added; tests and live smoke prove the candidate remains blocked.
close_reason: Action detail now renders per-action mutation readiness from the WILQ API; tests and live smoke show the WordPress draft candidate remains blocked.

title: Show WordPress draft write readiness in content workflow
description: Why this issue exists: WordPress draft write readiness existed in the API but was not visible where the operator works through content workflow. What needs to be done: surface the readiness state in /content-workflow so Wilku sees env, REST, audit requirements and blockers before any draft-only write path is considered.
design: Consume the existing WILQ API readiness endpoint. Do not enable writes or publication from the dashboard; show blockers and next safe step only.
acceptance_criteria: /content-workflow shows a marketer-readable WordPress draft write readiness panel, live writes remain blocked unless API readiness passes, and tests prove the panel renders without exposing raw API mechanics.
close_reason: Rendered WordPress draft write readiness in /content-workflow using the existing API endpoint; tests and live smoke confirm readiness is visible while writes remain blocked.

title: Expose first WordPress draft mutation candidate
description: Why this issue exists: WILQ has WordPress draft execution and mutation readiness, but no explicit apply-capable ActionObject candidate for the first safe draft-only write path. What needs to be done: expose a read-only candidate/readiness path that shows exactly why WordPress draft write is still blocked, without enabling publication or vendor writes.
design: Keep WILQ API canonical. Do not enable writes by default. If an apply-capable candidate is added, it must stay blocked unless ActionObject validation, preview, human review, confirmation, impact/readiness and WordPress draft write readiness all pass.
acceptance_criteria: WILQ shows a clear first mutation candidate for WordPress draft-only writes, readiness remains blocked without env/audit/adapter execution, and tests/live smoke prove no publication or vendor write can happen accidentally.
close_reason: Implemented aggregate mutation readiness first_write_candidate for WordPress draft-only activation; focused tests and live smoke prove vendor writes remain impossible.

title: Acquire real Ekologus ACF layout source
description: SSH to Ekologus works and main docroot is /var/www/vhosts/ekologus.pl/httpdocs. WILQ installed user-scoped WP-CLI and a read-only host wrapper at ~/.local/bin/wilq-wp-readonly outside the WordPress docroot; the wrapper uses Plesk PHP 8.3 with mysqli, blocks write-capable commands, and verified that ACF Pro is active. ACF field groups are visible via read-only WP-CLI, including Strona główna, Podstrona, Hero and Nasze usługi. Remaining work: extract typed ACF Flexible Content field/layout definitions from those field groups or an ACF export, then feed wordpress_authoring_profile_v1 so payload previews use real layouts.
acceptance_criteria: Live WILQ authoring profile has acf.layouts_discovered=true for wordpress_ekologus; each layout has field names/types/required flags/source_method; content workflow payload preview maps at least one live draft package to the discovered real layout without external write; tests or live proof cover no raw secrets, no publication and ActionObject write boundary.
close_reason: Completed real ACF layout source acquisition. WILQ installed host-side read-only WP-CLI guardrails, generated a local ignored ACF export from read-only acf-field-group/acf-field metadata, wired it into local .env, and live /api/content/wordpress/authoring-profile now reports acf.layouts_discovered=true with 21 layout-like sections and no blockers. Live authoring payload preview maps the current homepage draft package to the real podstrona ACF layout with external_write_attempted=false and leaves repeaters unmapped instead of pretending scalar content is safe.

title: WordPress Site Discovery and ACF Flexible Content contract
description: Design and implement the first generic WILQ WordPress authoring discovery layer for Ekologus and future clients. Start with WP REST API discovery, then ACF REST exposure if available, then WP-CLI over SSH only when REST cannot expose ACF field groups/flexible layouts, and only then a read-only mu-plugin/helper. Compile results into a typed site authoring profile for ACF Flexible Content layouts, not hardcoded Ekologus-only assumptions.
design: WILQ API remains canonical. Discovery is read-only and produces typed contracts: site version/capabilities, post types, taxonomies, media constraints, ACF flexible layout names, required/optional fields, field types, repeaters, CTA/link/image requirements and safe write capabilities. Draft building maps WILQ content packages to existing ACF sections. Writes remain ActionObject validate -> preview -> review -> confirm -> audit.
acceptance_criteria: API exposes a read-only WordPress site authoring profile for Ekologus; discovery records which method supplied each fact (REST, ACF REST, WP-CLI, helper); ACF Flexible Content layouts are represented in typed schema; WILQ can build a draft-only section payload preview without publishing; tests cover REST-first discovery, missing ACF fields, WP-CLI fallback as explicit readiness state, and no vendor write without ActionObject.
close_reason: Implemented read-only wordpress_authoring_profile_v1 and dry-run ACF payload preview contract. API exposes REST, ACF, WP-CLI/helper readiness and draft-only write boundary; tests cover REST-first, missing ACF, password-based WP-CLI readiness, broken WP-CLI state, ACF export typed layouts, endpoint responses and no vendor write. Live SSH proof found docroot /var/www/vhosts/ekologus.pl/httpdocs and broken /usr/local/bin/wp, so real layout discovery is tracked separately.

title: Social history discovery for LinkedIn and Facebook dedupe

acceptance_criteria: Knowledge card inventory includes reviewed service-specific cards and tests prove required service/CTA/claim/evidence cards block Sales Brief/draft when missing. Cards with only internal seeded lineage cannot unlock production-depth status.

title: Harden measurement outcome provenance for content decisions
description: Second-opinion follow-up after Goal 005 activation: verify and harden whether content measurement outcome interpretation is formally tied to metric_store facts, connector refresh/JobRun provenance, evidence IDs and the original content work item decision. Request typing for content POSTs is already fixed, so this task is about measurement provenance and false-success prevention, not Zod request schemas.
design: Keep outcome interpretation conservative. Do not create fake attribution science or SEO scores. Measurement hardening should make provenance explicit: which metric facts, connectors, refresh/job lineage, dates and work_item_id support an outcome. If live WILQ API is unreachable, work from local contracts/tests and record live-proof blocker.
acceptance_criteria: Audit or implementation proves one of: (a) outcome interpretation is tied to stored metric facts/job or connector refresh provenance with tests, or (b) a precise blocker states what metric_store/JobRun linkage is missing. Tests must prove no success/failure claim without metric evidence IDs, allowed metric names, source connectors, matching work_item_id/window and measurement readiness.
close_reason: Implemented fail-closed measurement outcome provenance with metric_fact_ids, refresh_run_ids, matching work item/window/url, connector and allowed metric checks plus focused tests.

title: Define evidence-based draft variant selection guard
description: Goal 004 can produce draft variants, but Wilku still needs to understand which variant is safest/useful and why. Define a bounded selection/recommendation guard so variant choice is based on evidence coverage, service fit, buyer problem, CTA fit, duplicate risk and quality review findings, not the prettiest text.
design: Do not create a fake SEO score or new generation flow. API should expose variant comparison as blockers/dimensions/safe next step. React only renders the API-owned comparison.
acceptance_criteria: A design or implementation task exists for variant comparison with no magic score, no publish-ready claim, and tests planned/proven for generic-SEO variant blocking and evidence-bound recommended variant selection.
close_reason: Implemented API-owned draft variant selection guard with evidence dimensions and focused tests

title: Audit Sales Brief v2 signal quality
description: Assess whether enrichment inputs for Sales Brief v2 are dense, fresh and useful enough for real Ekologus content decisions. Separate connector freshness issues from model/card interpretation issues.
design: No fake SEO scores. Use WILQ evidence/source connector metadata, GSC/GA4/Ahrefs/WordPress signal density, service fit, buyer problem, CTA hypothesis and measurement baseline. Weak evidence must become a typed blocker or low-confidence state, not a polished generic brief.
acceptance_criteria: Audit identifies for current queue candidates whether signal quality is sufficient, thin, stale or blocked; names the weak connector/domain cause; and defines exact API/test changes needed before claiming daily content usefulness.
close_reason: Completed Sales Brief v2 signal quality audit, fixed enrichment source-fact lineage and filed follow-ups for missing knowledge cards/blocked snapshot

acceptance_criteria: A concrete API/dashboard design is ready for a read-only Service Profile with card status, source lineage, freshness, coverage gaps and flag-for-review semantics. It explicitly blocks direct knowledge writes and says what tests will prove it.
